commonMedications.name: Semaglutida / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Limited FONASA/mutualista coverage. Cash price at pharmacies.

commonMedications.name: Levotiroxina [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: FONASA mutualista covers with small copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Farmashop, Farmacia San Roque, and independent farmacias. Prescription required for most medications. Generics available but selection smaller than larger Latin American markets.
medicine.notes: Uruguay FONASA provides healthcare through mutualistas (private non-profit providers). Legal residents must enroll (4.5-8% of income). Medication coverage varies by mutualista plan. Uruguay is more expensive than other Latin American countries.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Uruguay at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Antel Postpaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~UY$450/line (~$11). Antel is state telecom, best coverage.

items.name: Ground Beef / Turkey
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Fish (local catch)
items.notes: Fresh local fish often cheaper than US

items.name: Coffee (ground/beans)
items.notes: Excellent local coffee in most LATAM countries

items.name: Premium Dog Food (kibble)
items.notes: Imported premium brands cost more; local brands available

breakdown.notes: Gastos comunes (common expenses) typically 10-15% of rent in apartments. Houses may not have this fee.

housing.taxNotes: No VAT on residential rent. IRPF (income tax on rent) is landlord responsibility. Rents often denominated in USD or indexed to Unidad Indexada (UI).
housing.leaseTerms: 24-month minimum lease by law. Security deposit 2-3 months rent. Garantia de alquiler (rental guarantee) required from bank or insurance company.
housing.marketNotes: Montevideo most expensive. Punta del Este seasonal (summer premium Dec-Feb). Colonia del Sacramento quieter and cheaper.

stores.name: Disco
stores.notes: Major chain with wide product range

stores.name: Ta-Ta
stores.notes: Budget-friendly chain, largest network in Uruguay

healthcare.coverageSummary: FONASA/mutualista enrollment mandatory for residents. Contributions income-based (~3-8%). Covers comprehensive care. Private supplemental available.

gpVisit.notes: Mutualista covers GP with small copay (ticket moderador). Good primary care network.

specialist.notes: Specialist referral through mutualista. Private specialists $50-80.

privateInsurance.notes: BlueCross, ASSE, or mutualista upgrade. International plans available.

emergency.notes: Emergency care covered by mutualista. Hospital Britanico well-regarded for expats.

dental.notes: Mutualista covers basic dental. Private dental affordable by US standards.

healthcare.notes: Uruguay has a well-developed healthcare system. Hospital Britanico in Montevideo is popular with English-speaking expats. Good pharmacies throughout.

womensCut.notes: Good salons in Montevideo and Punta del Este

gymMonthly.notes: Smart Fit, local gyms in Punta del Este, Uruguay

seniorDiscounts.programName: BPS (Banco de Prevision Social) Senior Benefits

discounts.name: BPS Pension
discounts.description: BPS provides retirement pensions based on contributions. Minimum pension guaranteed for residents with sufficient contribution years.

discounts.name: ASSE Healthcare
discounts.description: ASSE public healthcare system available to all residents. Seniors can access through mutualistas with income-based contributions.

discounts.name: Transit Discounts
discounts.description: Reduced public transit fares for seniors in Montevideo (STM card). Free transport for some low-income seniors.

discounts.name: Cultural Access
discounts.description: Reduced or free entry to museums, cultural events, and public recreation programs for seniors.

discounts.name: Pharmacy Benefits
discounts.description: FONASA covers medications through mutualista pharmacy network. Co-pays reduced for common medications.

monthlyPass.notes: STM rechargeable card system in Montevideo. 1-hour transfer window.

publicTransit.coverage: Bus network in Montevideo (STM card). Intercity buses connect cities.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Reduced fares for seniors on public transit. STM card with senior discount.

rideShare.availability: Uber and Cabify available in Montevideo. Taxis affordable with meters.
rideShare.notes: Ride-share widely used in Punta Del Este. Negotiate taxi fares before riding if no meter.

registration.notes: Vehicle registration through local transit authority in Punta Del Este.

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. International driving permit accepted for first 90 days.

fuel.notes: Fuel prices vary by region. Government may regulate prices.

parking.notes: Street parking available in Punta Del Este. Malls and supermarkets usually have free parking.

tolls.notes: Toll roads on major highways between cities.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior car discounts. Patente (vehicle tax) varies by department.

transportation.walkability: Good in Montevideo and Punta del Este. Car needed elsewhere.

cycling.infrastructure: Bicycle infrastructure varies in Punta Del Este. Urban areas gradually improving cycling lanes.
cycling.notes: Heat and terrain may limit cycling as primary transport.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Small but growing EV market. UTE (state utility) expanding charging network. Import costs may apply.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Reduced import duties on EVs. IMESI tax exemption for electric vehicles.

chargingHome.notes: Home charging feasible in houses and some apartments in Punta Del Este.

chargingPublic.notes: Public charging network growing in Uruguay. Check availability in Punta Del Este.

insurance.notes: EV insurance through major local providers.

maintenance.notes: EV service availability growing. Authorized dealers in major cities.

registration.notes: Reduced or exempt registration for EVs.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard warranty. Service access best in major cities.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV resale market developing in Uruguay. Larger cities have better resale prospects.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging, insurance, maintenance (monthly avg), registration. Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.
